title: National Parents' Day: Be the Role Model Your Children Need
h1: Univision US: National Parents’ Day: Cómo ser el ejemplo que tus hijos necesitan
metaDescription: National Parents' Day honors the love, guidance, and role models parents provide in shaping children’s values. Find out how parents can lead by example.
excerpt: National Parents’ Day, celebrated annually on the fourth Sunday of July in the United States, honors the dedication, love, and guidance parents provide in raising strong, value-driven children. This year, the observance

blocks.text: National Parents’ Day, celebrated annually on the fourth Sunday of July in the United States, honors the dedication, love, and guidance parents provide in raising strong, value-driven children. This year, the observance falls on July 27, 2025. Parents face the complex task of guiding their children through social media pressures, political division, and mental health concerns, while fostering resilience and hope.

Experts emphasize that children learn more from parents’ actions than words. Establishing daily routines, developing consistent respect, modeling perseverance, and teaching other skills help children develop emotional intelligence and ethical values. However, achieving this balance can be challenging as parents navigate work responsibilities and family life, making presence and engagement at home as important as providing materially.

National Parents’ Day serves as a reminder to prioritize meaningful time together, whether through shared hobbies, outdoor activities, storytelling, or simply being present and attentive. The celebration recognizes diverse family structures and that effective parenting comes in many forms.

Drawing on his experience as a father of three, William Stern , Founder of Cardiff emphasizes the importance of leading by example, noting that children internalize behaviors more than verbal advice. He shared that being genuinely present, whether at work or at home, is key to providing children with the guidance and values they need.

Cardiff’s involvement reflects its broader mission of fostering responsibility and thoughtful decision-making, extending its ethos of leadership and commitment beyond business into family and community life. By highlighting Stern’s perspective, the initiative demonstrates how principled leadership in everyday life can shape the next generation.
